title: ResNet & Deep CNN Architectures
summary: ResNet introduced residual connections to CNNs — the same concept used in transformers. Enabled training of 100+ layer networks. Key insight: residuals are not specific to transformers, they are a general architectural principle for deep networks. Also covers: VGG (depth matters), Inception (multi-scale), depthwise separable convolutions (efficiency).
key_insights: Residual connections originated here (2015) — transformers borrowed them (2017)
Same principle: X_out = X + F(X). Identity shortcut prevents gradient vanishing in deep stacks
Depthwise separable convolutions: factorize standard conv into depthwise + pointwise for efficiency (MobileNet)
Batch normalization (BN): normalizes across batch dimension — contrast with LayerNorm which normalizes across features

mathematical_core.residual_block: X_out = X + F(X, W) where F = conv → BN → ReLU → conv → BN
mathematical_core.bn_vs_ln: BN: normalize across batch dimension (same feature, different samples). LN: normalize across feature dimension (same sample, different features).

notes: Residual connections insight from transformer discussion directly transfers here
